Notes: Published brief did not identify whether the game was home or away.
RecapTitle: Frederica Academy shuts out St. Andrew's in season opener
Recap:
Frederica Academy defeated St. Andrew's 4-0 in the first published game of the Spring 2004 girls soccer season. Becca Candler scored twice for Frederica, while Paige Day and Jennifer Brian added goals. Day also recorded an assist.

Mary Wilkowski kept St. Andrew's in the match with nine saves. Frederica goalkeeper Shalene Cutia made four saves as the Knights opened 1-0 and St. Andrew's moved to 0-1.

Notes: Published brief did not identify whether the game was home or away. The published scoring line accounts for eight of St. Andrew's nine goals.
RecapTitle: St. Andrew's opens region play with shutout of Colleton Prep
Recap:
St. Andrew's defeated Colleton Prep 9-0 in a SCISA 2-AA region match on March 11, 2004. Jennifer Moesch scored four goals, while Megan Johnson, Maggie Hinchey, Emily Dunn, and Becca Timms also scored. The published scoring line accounts for eight of St. Andrew's nine goals.

Leighanne Evans supplied three assists, and Emily Aimone added one. Mary Wilkowski made two saves for the shutout as St. Andrew's improved to 1-1 overall and 1-0 in region play.

Venue: Mount De Sales High School, Macon
Notes: Cavalier Classic at Mount De Sales High School in Macon. Reported in the March 20, 2004 Savannah Morning News.
RecapTitle: Moesch scores twice as St. Andrew's shuts out Dominion Christian
Recap:
St. Andrew's defeated Dominion Christian 2-0 at the Cavalier Classic at Mount De Sales High School in Macon. Jennifer Moesch scored both goals for St. Andrew's, Leighanne Evans added an assist, and Mary Wilkowski made three saves in the shutout.

The result moved St. Andrew's to 2-1, while Dominion Christian fell to 2-2.

Venue: Mount De Sales High School, Macon
Notes: Cavalier Classic at Mount De Sales High School in Macon. Date assigned from the March 20, 2004 Savannah Morning News report sequence.
RecapTitle: Moesch goal sends St. Andrew's past First Presbyterian Day
Recap:
St. Andrew's defeated First Presbyterian Day 1-0 at the Cavalier Classic at Mount De Sales High School in Macon. Jennifer Moesch scored the game's only goal, and Mary Wilkowski made 10 saves for another shutout.

The win improved St. Andrew's to 3-1 and handed First Presbyterian Day its first listed loss at 4-1.
RecordNote: Records: St. Andrew's 3-1, First Presbyterian Day 4-1

Venue: Mount De Sales High School, Macon
Notes: Mount DeSales Cavalier Classic semifinal at Macon.
RecapTitle: Frederica Academy tops St. Andrew's in Cavalier Classic semifinal
Recap:
Frederica Academy defeated St. Andrew's 5-0 in the Cavalier Classic semifinal at Mount De Sales High School in Macon.

The March 21 report listed only the final score from the semifinal.

Venue: Mount De Sales High School, Macon
Notes: St. Andrew's placed third out of the 16 teams competing in the GISA tournament. Jennifer Moesch and Mary Wilkowski were named to the all-tournament team.
RecapTitle: St. Andrew's edges Fellowship Christian for third place
Recap:
St. Andrew's defeated Fellowship Christian 3-2 in the Cavalier Classic third-place game at Mount De Sales High School in Macon. Jennifer Moesch scored twice and Leighanne Evans added a goal for St. Andrew's.

Mary Wilkowski made eight saves for St. Andrew's, while Fellowship Christian goalkeeper Jennifer Wilson made five saves. The March 21 report listed St. Andrew's record at 5-2.

St. Andrew's placed third out of the 16 teams competing in the GISA tournament. Jennifer Moesch and Mary Wilkowski were named to the all-tournament team.

RecapTitle: St. Andrew's shuts out The King's Academy in Kelme Tournament
Recap:
St. Andrew's defeated The King's Academy 6-0 in the Kelme Tournament of Champions. Jennifer Moesch and Becca Timms scored two goals apiece, while Maggie Hinchey and Leighanne Evans added one goal each.

Moesch also recorded an assist, and Mary Wilkowski made two saves for St. Andrew's.

RecapTitle: Moesch hat trick leads St. Andrew's past Trinity Collegiate
Recap:
St. Andrew's defeated Trinity Collegiate 4-1 in the Kelme Tournament of Champions. Jennifer Moesch scored three goals and Maggie Hinchey added one for St. Andrew's, while Alyssa Pignone scored for Trinity Collegiate.

Hinchey and Grace Wilkowski recorded assists for St. Andrew's, and Mary Wilkowski made three saves.

RecapTitle: Moesch hat trick powers region win over Beaufort Academy
Recap:
St. Andrew's defeated Beaufort Academy 5-0 in a SCISA Region 2-AA match on March 29, 2004. Jennifer Moesch scored three goals and Leighanne Evans scored twice for St. Andrew's.

Evans added one assist, Moesch added two assists, and Mary Wilkowski made two saves as St. Andrew's improved to 7-2 overall and 2-0 in SCISA Region 2-AA.

RecapTitle: Haggberg scores twice as St. Andrew's beats Colleton Prep
Recap:
St. Andrew's defeated Colleton Prep 5-1 in a GISA Region 2-AA match on April 1, 2004. Layne Haggberg scored twice, and Leighanne Evans, Jennifer Moesch, and Rebecca Timms each added a goal for St. Andrew's. Whitney Smith scored for Colleton Prep.

Evans, Maggie Hinchey, and Moesch recorded assists for St. Andrew's. Mary Wilkowski made two saves and Stephanie Vine made four saves as St. Andrew's improved to 8-2 overall and 3-0 in GISA Region 2-AA.

RecapTitle: Moesch scores three as St. Andrew's beats Trinity Collegiate
Recap:
St. Andrew's defeated Trinity Collegiate 5-1 in a non-region match on April 2, 2004. Jennifer Moesch scored three goals, while Megan Johnson and Grace Wilkowski also scored for St. Andrew's. Whitney Jenkins scored for Trinity Collegiate.

Rebecca Timms recorded two assists, Emily Aimone added one, and Moesch added two assists. The published brief listed St. Andrew's at 8-2 and Trinity Collegiate at 4-4.

RecapTitle: Wilkowski saves 12 in scoreless draw with Hilton Head Christian
Recap:
St. Andrew's and Hilton Head Christian played to a 0-0 draw on April 6, 2004.

Mary Wilkowski made 12 saves for St. Andrew's as the Lions moved to 9-2-1. Hilton Head Christian was listed at 12-5-1.
RecordNote: Records: St. Andrew's 9-2-1, Hilton Head Christian 12-5-1

Notes: St. Andrew's clinched the SCISA Region 1-AA championship.
RecapTitle: St. Andrew's clinches region title with shutout of Beaufort Academy
Recap:
St. Andrew's defeated Beaufort Academy 5-0 on April 29, 2004. Jennifer Moesch scored three goals, while Grace Wilkowski and Becca Timms also scored for St. Andrew's.

Emily Aimone recorded an assist, and Mary Wilkowski made six saves for St. Andrew's. Beaufort Academy was credited with five saves. The report listed St. Andrew's record at 10-3.

St. Andrew's clinched the SCISA Region 1-AA championship.

Notes: SCISA Class AA/A state quarterfinal. St. Andrew's advanced to host Lowcountry Day School in the semifinals.
RecapTitle: St. Andrew's girls in SCISA semifinals
Recap:
ST. ANDREW'S GIRLS IN SCISA SEMIFINALS

Entering Tuesday's SCISA Class AA/A state girls' soccer quarterfinal, St. Andrew's second-year coach Mark Gibbons was concerned with changing the direction of the ball and better positioning to give his powerful offense more scoring opportunities. His wish was granted as the Saints scored eight goals and attempted 31 shots en route to a 8-0 defeat of The King's Academy, of Florence, S.C.

The scoring began early as senior Jennifer Moesch took a nice pass from sophomore Grace Wilkowski and found the back of the net four minutes into the match. The bombardment of The King's Academy's goal continued midway through the first half as the Saints scored three goals in a five-minute span. Two were by sophomore Becca Timms, one in the 21st minute and the other off a perfect lead pass from LeighAnne Evans in the 23rd minute.

Moesch added another goal minutes later, giving the Saints a 4-0 lead. Sophomore Emily Aimone added the fifth and final goal of the first half with five minutes remaining.

The Saints' defense led by senior Layne Haggberg, junior Megan Johnson and freshmen Claire Oosterhaudt and Sarah Lundgren stifled every chance The King's Academy had all afternoon. Not one shot on goal was taken against the Saints' goalkeepers.

The Saints' scoring continued in the second half as Brittany McLaughlin, Meghan McCarthey and Emily Aimone each tallied a goal.

"We did what we had to do," Gibbons said. "Now, we have to get past a tough Lowcountry team who we had trouble with last year. Our game Thursday will measure our season for us. We want to have a shot at the state championship."

The Saints will host Lowcountry Day School, of Pawley's Island, S.C., at 5 p.m. Thursday in a semifinal. Lowcountry beat Beaufort Academy in last year's state final after knocking off St. Andrew's 1-0 in a semifinal.

Notes: SCISA Class AA/A state semifinal. St. Andrew's advanced to the state championship at Heathwood Hall School in Columbia.
RecapTitle: St. Andrew's advances to state championship
Recap:
St. Andrew's has been known for its physical play all year long. The Saints' toughness had Lowcountry Day coach Danny Ball worried before Thursday's SCISA Class AA/A semifinal contest. His concerns became a reality as St. Andrew's trounced Lowcountry 5-0 to earn a spot in the state championship Saturday in Columbia, S.C.

The Saints will face the Trinity Christian-Greenwood Christian winner for the state championship at 2:30 p.m. Saturday at Heathwood Hall School.

Against Lowcountry Day, St. Andrew's did what Ball feared: dominated the midfield and had the upper hand physically.

"We have to watch out for Jennifer Moesch, a tough, physical player, and control the midfield," Lowcountry's coach said before the match.

Ball would not comment after the Saints' victory.

Moesch, a senior, tallied four goals and senior LeighAnne Evans added one as the Saints' dominated possession all afternoon against the visitors from Pawley's Island, S.C., piling up 24 shots.

The scoring began five minutes into the game as Moesch took an excellent pass from Evans in the Marlins' box and fired it into the right corner of the goal to take a 1-0 lead. Minutes later, the same duo added another goal as Moesch took the ball down the right side and crossed the ball to a waiting Evans.

The Marlins' slowed down the Saints' attack for nearly 30 minutes until sophomore Becca Timms dropped off a pass to Moesch, who converted her second goal of the day to push the lead to 3-0.

Saints coach Mark Gibbons wanted his team to have a stout midfield presence, and it did. Seniors Brittany McLaughlin and Layne Haggberg controlled the midfield throughout the contest, finding open teammates, which led to numerous scoring opportunities.

The Saints' defense, which is made up of freshmen Mary Wilkowski and Sarah Lundgren, sophomore Maggie Hinchey and junior Megan Johnson, stifled Lowcountry's offense, holding it to five shots on goal.

"The first half we played well, but we played with much more confidence in the second half," Gibbons said.

"I am proud of the way our girls came out against a good Lowcountry team. We got ourselves where we wanted. Now, we just have to finish and take home the championship."

Notes: SCISA Class A/AA state final. Greenwood Christian won on penalty kicks after regulation, two 10-minute overtimes, two five-minute overtimes, and sudden death.
RecapTitle: St. Andrew's finishes as state runner-up
Recap:
In the SCISA Class A/AA girls' soccer final at the same field, Greenwood Christian beat St. Andrew's 2-1 on penalty kicks.

The Saints controlled play for most of the match, outshooting Greenwood Christian 36-26, but were unable to put away several good scoring opportunities.

Greenwood Christian took a 1-0 lead when Joy Kane scored with 5:14 remaining in regulation, but St. Andrew's tied the score on Jennifer Moesch's goal with 2:32 left in regulation.

The teams played a pair of 10-minute and five-minute overtimes before they went to penalty kicks. After the first five penalty kicks, both teams scored twice.

Greenwood Christian's Mallory Rose scored her team's first penalty kick in sudden death. Saints goalkeeper Mary Wilkowski was able to get a hand on the shot, but the officials ruled the ball went over the goal line.
